<content>1: // ─── SSE Event Wiring ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Extracted from relay-stack.ts: the pipeline that takes SSE events from
3: // OpenCode, translates them, filters by session, records to cache, broadcasts
4: // to browser clients, and sends push notifications.
5: 
6: import type { Translator } from "./event-translator.js";
7: import type { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
8: import {
9: 	hasInfoWithSessionID,
10: 	hasPartWithSessionID,
11: 	hasSessionID,
12: 	isPermissionRepliedEvent,
13: 	isSessionErrorEvent,
14: } from "./opencode-events.js";
15: import type { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
16: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
17: import type { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
18: import type { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
19: import type { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
20: import type { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
21: import type { ToolContentStore } from "./tool-content-store.js";
22: import { truncateToolResult } from "./truncate-content.js";
23: import type { OpenCodeEvent, RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
24: 
25: // ─── Session ID extraction ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
26: // OpenCode SSE events store sessionID in different locations by event type:
27: //   - Top-level: message.part.delta, session.status, message.part.removed, etc.
28: //   - Nested in part: message.part.updated → properties.part.sessionID
29: //   - Nested in info: message.updated → properties.info.sessionID
30: // We must check all locations to correctly attribute events to sessions.
31: 
32: export function extractSessionId(event: OpenCodeEvent): string | undefined {
33: 	const props = event.properties;
34: 	// 1. Top-level sessionID (most common)
35: 	if (hasSessionID(props)) {
36: 		return props.sessionID;
37: 	}
38: 	// 2. Nested in part (message.part.updated)
39: 	if (hasPartWithSessionID(props)) {
40: 		return props.part.sessionID;
41: 	}
42: 	// 3. Nested in info (message.updated, session.updated)
43: 	if (hasInfoWithSessionID(props)) {
44: 		return props.info.sessionID ?? props.info.id;
45: 	}
46: 	return undefined;
47: }
48: 
49: // ─── Cache-eligible event types ──────────────────────────────────────────────
50: // Only chat-relevant events should be recorded to the message cache.
51: // Non-chat events (file_changed, permission_*, todo_state, etc.) would create
52: // false-positive cache hits that skip the REST API fallback on session switch,
53: // causing pre-relay conversation history to be lost.
54: 
55: const CACHEABLE_TYPES = new Set([
56: 	"user_message",
57: 	"delta",
58: 	"thinking_start",
59: 	"thinking_delta",
60: 	"thinking_stop",
61: 	"tool_start",
62: 	"tool_executing",
63: 	"tool_result",
64: 	"result",
65: 	"status",
66: 	"done",
67: 	"error",
68: ]);
69: 
70: export function isCacheable(msg: { type: string }): boolean {
71: 	return CACHEABLE_TYPES.has(msg.type);
72: }
73: 
74: // ─── SSE Wiring Dependencies ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
75: 
76: export interface SSEWiringDeps {
77: 	translator: Translator;
78: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
79: 	messageCache: MessageCache;
80: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
81: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
82: 	overrides: SessionOverrides;
83: 	toolContentStore: ToolContentStore;
84: 	wsHandler: {
85: 		broadcast: (msg: RelayMessage) => void;
86: 	};
87: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
88: 	log: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
89: }
90: 
91: // ─── Handle a single SSE event ───────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: export function handleSSEEvent(
94: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
95: 	event: OpenCodeEvent,
96: ): void {
97: 	const {
98: 		translator,
99: 		sessionMgr,
100: 		messageCache,
101: 		permissionBridge,
102: 		questionBridge,
103: 		overrides,
104: 		toolContentStore,
105: 		wsHandler,
106: 		pushManager,
107: 		log,
108: 	} = deps;
109: 
110: 	const eventSessionId = extractSessionId(event);
111: 	log(`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"}`);
112: 
113: 	// ── Permission / question bridge routing ──────────────────────────────
114: 
115: 	if (event.type === "permission.asked") {
116: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionRequest(event);
117: 		if (pushManager) {
118: 			const id =
119: 				typeof event.properties.id === "string"
120: 					? event.properties.id
121: 					: "unknown";
122: 			const tool =
123: 				typeof event.properties.tool === "string"
124: 					? event.properties.tool
125: 					: typeof event.properties.permission === "string"
126: 						? event.properties.permission
127: 						: "A tool";
128: 			pushManager
129: 				.sendToAll({
130: 					type: "permission_request",
131: 					title: "Permission Needed",
132: 					body: `${tool} needs approval`,
133: 					tag: `perm-${id}`,
134: 				})
135: 				.catch(() => {});
136: 		}
137: 	}
138: 	if (event.type === "question.asked") {
139: 		questionBridge.onQuestion(event);
140: 		if (pushManager) {
141: 			pushManager
142: 				.sendToAll({
143: 					type: "ask_user",
144: 					title: "Question from Claude",
145: 					body: "Claude has a question for you",
146: 					tag: "opencode-ask",
147: 				})
148: 				.catch(() => {});
149: 		}
150: 	}
151: 	if (isPermissionRepliedEvent(event)) {
152: 		permissionBridge.onPermissionReplied(event.properties.id);
153: 	}
154: 
155: 	// ── Log session errors for debugging ──────────────────────────────────
156: 
157: 	if (isSessionErrorEvent(event)) {
158: 		const err = event.properties.error;
159: 		log(
160: 			`   [sse] event=${event.type} session=${eventSessionId ?? "?"} Session error: ${err?.name ?? "?"} — ${err?.data?.message ?? "(no message)"}`,
161: 		);
162: 	}
163: 
164: 	// ── Translate → filter → cache → broadcast ───────────────────────────
165: 
166: 	const messages = translator.translate(event);
167: 	if (!messages) return;
168: 
169: 	const activeId = sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId();
170: 	const isActiveSession = !eventSessionId || eventSessionId === activeId;
171: 
172: 	const toSend = Array.isArray(messages) ? messages : [messages];
173: 	for (let msg of toSend) {
174: 		// Clear processing timeout when done event arrives for active session
175: 		if (msg.type === "done" && isActiveSession) {
176: 			overrides.clearProcessingTimeout();
177: 		}
178: 
179: 		// Truncate large tool_result content before caching and broadcasting
180: 		if (msg.type === "tool_result") {
181: 			const { truncated, fullContent } = truncateToolResult(msg);
182: 			if (fullContent !== undefined) {
183: 				const sessionId = eventSessionId ?? activeId ?? undefined;
184: 				toolContentStore.store(msg.id, fullContent, sessionId);
185: 			}
186: 			msg = truncated;
187: 		}
188: 
189: 		// Only record chat-relevant events to the cache
190: 		const recordId = eventSessionId ?? activeId;
191: 		if (recordId && isCacheable(msg)) {
192: 			messageCache.recordEvent(recordId, msg);
193: 		}
194: 
195: 		// Only broadcast events for the active session to browser clients
196: 		if (!isActiveSession) continue;
197: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
198: 
199: 		// Push notifications for done/error events
200: 		if (pushManager) {
201: 			if (msg.type === "done") {
202: 				pushManager
203: 					.sendToAll({
204: 						type: "done",
205: 						title: "Task Complete",
206: 						body: "Claude has finished processing.",
207: 						tag: "opencode-done",
208: 					})
209: 					.catch(() => {});
210: 			}
211: 			if (msg.type === "error") {
212: 				pushManager
213: 					.sendToAll({
214: 						type: "error",
215: 						title: "Error",
216: 						body: msg.message || "An error occurred",
217: 						tag: "opencode-error",
218: 					})
219: 					.catch(() => {});
220: 			}
221: 		}
222: 	}
223: }
224: 
225: // ─── Wire all SSE consumer event listeners ───────────────────────────────────
226: 
227: export function wireSSEConsumer(
228: 	deps: SSEWiringDeps,
229: 	consumer: SSEConsumer,
230: ): void {
231: 	const { log } = deps;
232: 
233: 	consumer.on("connected", () => {
234: 		log(
235: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Connected to OpenCode event stream`,
236: 		);
237: 		deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
238: 			type: "connection_status",
239: 			status: "connected",
240: 		});
241: 	});
242: 	consumer.on("disconnected", (err) => {
243: 		log(
244: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Disconnected${err ? `: ${err.message}` : ""}`,
245: 		);
246: 		deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
247: 			type: "connection_status",
248: 			status: "disconnected",
249: 		});
250: 	});
251: 	consumer.on("reconnecting", ({ attempt, delay }) => {
252: 		log(
253: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Reconnecting (attempt ${attempt}, ${delay}ms delay)…`,
254: 		);
255: 		deps.wsHandler.broadcast({
256: 			type: "connection_status",
257: 			status: "reconnecting",
258: 		});
259: 	});
260: 	consumer.on("error", (err) =>
261: 		log(
262: 			`   [sse] session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Error: ${err.message}`,
263: 		),
264: 	);
265: 
266: 	consumer.on("event", (event: OpenCodeEvent) => {
267: 		handleSSEEvent(deps, event);
268: 	});
269: }

<content>1: // ─── WebSocket Message Router (Ticket 2.2) ──────────────────────────────────
2: // Pure logic for routing incoming WebSocket messages to correct handlers,
3: // client tracking, broadcast targeting, and state snapshot building.
4: // Deliberately IO-free: no actual WebSocket I/O — just routing decisions.
5: 
6: import type { RelayMessage } from "./types.js";
7: 
8: // ─── Message types from browser ──────────────────────────────────────────────
9: 
10: export type IncomingMessageType =
11: 	| "message"
12: 	| "permission_response"
13: 	| "ask_user_response"
14: 	| "question_reject"
15: 	| "new_session"
16: 	| "switch_session"
17: 	| "delete_session"
18: 	| "rename_session"
19: 	| "list_sessions"
20: 	| "search_sessions"
21: 	| "load_more_history"
22: 	| "terminal_command"
23: 	| "input_sync"
24: 	| "switch_agent"
25: 	| "switch_model"
26: 	| "get_todo"
27: 	| "get_agents"
28: 	| "get_models"
29: 	| "get_commands"
30: 	| "get_projects"
31: 	| "add_project"
32: 	| "get_file_list"
33: 	| "get_file_content"
34: 	| "get_tool_content"
35: 	| "pty_create"
36: 	| "pty_input"
37: 	| "pty_resize"
38: 	| "pty_close"
39: 	| "cancel"
40: 	| "rewind";
41: 
42: const VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES = new Set<string>([
43: 	"message",
44: 	"permission_response",
45: 	"ask_user_response",
46: 	"question_reject",
47: 	"new_session",
48: 	"switch_session",
49: 	"delete_session",
50: 	"rename_session",
51: 	"list_sessions",
52: 	"search_sessions",
53: 	"load_more_history",
54: 	"terminal_command",
55: 	"input_sync",
56: 	"switch_agent",
57: 	"switch_model",
58: 	"get_todo",
59: 	"get_agents",
60: 	"get_models",
61: 	"get_commands",
62: 	"get_projects",
63: 	"add_project",
64: 	"get_file_list",
65: 	"get_file_content",
66: 	"get_tool_content",
67: 	"pty_create",
68: 	"pty_input",
69: 	"pty_resize",
70: 	"pty_close",
71: 	"cancel",
72: 	"rewind",
73: ]);
74: 
75: export interface IncomingMessage {
76: 	type: string;
77: 	[key: string]: unknown;
78: }
79: 
80: export interface RouteResult {
81: 	handler: IncomingMessageType;
82: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>;
83: }
84: 
85: export interface ErrorResult {
86: 	type: "error";
87: 	code: string;
88: 	message: string;
89: }
90: 
91: // ─── Message routing ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
92: 
93: /**
94:  * Parse and validate an incoming WebSocket message.
95:  * Returns the parsed object or null if parsing fails.
96:  */
97: export function parseIncomingMessage(raw: string): IncomingMessage | null {
98: 	try {
99: 		const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
100: 		if (
101: 			typeof parsed !== "object" ||
102: 			parsed === null ||
103: 			Array.isArray(parsed)
104: 		) {
105: 			return null;
106: 		}
107: 		if (typeof parsed.type !== "string") {
108: 			return null;
109: 		}
110: 		return parsed as IncomingMessage;
111: 	} catch {
112: 		return null;
113: 	}
114: }
115: 
116: /**
117:  * Route a parsed message to its handler.
118:  * Returns either a RouteResult or an ErrorResult for unknown types.
119:  */
120: export function routeMessage(msg: IncomingMessage): RouteResult | ErrorResult {
121: 	if (!VALID_MESSAGE_TYPES.has(msg.type)) {
122: 		return {
123: 			type: "error",
124: 			code: "UNKNOWN_MESSAGE_TYPE",
125: 			message: `Unknown message type: ${msg.type}`,
126: 		};
127: 	}
128: 
129: 	const { type, ...payload } = msg;
130: 	return {
131: 		handler: type as IncomingMessageType,
132: 		payload,
133: 	};
134: }
135: 
136: /**
137:  * Check if a route result is an error.
138:  */
139: export function isRouteError(
140: 	result: RouteResult | ErrorResult,
141: ): result is ErrorResult {
142: 	return "type" in result && (result as ErrorResult).type === "error";
143: }
144: 
145: // ─── Client tracking ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
146: 
147: export interface ClientTracker {
148: 	addClient(clientId: string): number;
149: 	removeClient(clientId: string): number;
150: 	getClientCount(): number;
151: 	getClientIds(): string[];
152: 	hasClient(clientId: string): boolean;
153: 	getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[];
154: }
155: 
156: /**
157:  * Create a client tracker for managing connected WebSocket clients.
158:  */
159: export function createClientTracker(): ClientTracker {
160: 	const clients = new Set<string>();
161: 
162: 	return {
163: 		addClient(clientId: string): number {
164: 			clients.add(clientId);
165: 			return clients.size;
166: 		},
167: 
168: 		removeClient(clientId: string): number {
169: 			clients.delete(clientId);
170: 			return clients.size;
171: 		},
172: 
173: 		getClientCount(): number {
174: 			return clients.size;
175: 		},
176: 
177: 		getClientIds(): string[] {
178: 			return [...clients];
179: 		},
180: 
181: 		hasClient(clientId: string): boolean {
182: 			return clients.has(clientId);
183: 		},
184: 
185: 		getBroadcastTargets(excludeClientId?: string): string[] {
186: 			if (!excludeClientId) return [...clients];
187: 			return [...clients].filter((id) => id !== excludeClientId);
188: 		},
189: 	};
190: }
191: 
192: // ─── State snapshot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
193: 
194: export interface SessionState {
195: 	sessionId: string;
196: 	title: string;
197: 	model?: string;
198: 	provider?: string;
199: 	status: "idle" | "processing";
200: 	clientCount: number;
201: }
202: 
203: export interface PendingState {
204: 	permissions: Array<{ requestId: string; toolName: string }>;
205: 	questions: Array<{ toolId: string; questions: unknown[] }>;
206: }
207: 
208: export interface StateSnapshot {
209: 	session: SessionState;
210: 	pending: PendingState;
211: 	clientCount: number;
212: }
213: 
214: /**
215:  * Build a state snapshot for a newly connected client.
216:  * The snapshot includes the session state, pending interactions, and client count.
217:  */
218: export function buildStateSnapshot(
219: 	session: SessionState,
220: 	pending: PendingState,
221: 	clientCount: number,
222: ): StateSnapshot {
223: 	return {
224: 		session: { ...session, clientCount },
225: 		pending: {
226: 			permissions: [...pending.permissions],
227: 			questions: [...pending.questions],
228: 		},
229: 		clientCount,
230: 	};
231: }
232: 
233: /**
234:  * Build the list of messages to send to a newly connected client.
235:  * Returns RelayMessage[] in the correct order per AC5.
236:  */
237: export function buildNewClientMessages(
238: 	snapshot: StateSnapshot,
239: ): RelayMessage[] {
240: 	const messages: RelayMessage[] = [];
241: 
242: 	// 1. Status
243: 	messages.push({
244: 		type: "status",
245: 		status: snapshot.session.status,
246: 	});
247: 
248: 	// 2. Client count
249: 	messages.push({
250: 		type: "client_count",
251: 		count: snapshot.clientCount,
252: 	});
253: 
254: 	// 3. Pending permissions
255: 	for (const perm of snapshot.pending.permissions) {
256: 		messages.push({
257: 			type: "permission_request",
258: 			requestId: perm.requestId,
259: 			toolName: perm.toolName,
260: 			toolInput: {},
261: 		});
262: 	}
263: 
264: 	// 4. Pending questions
265: 	for (const q of snapshot.pending.questions) {
266: 		messages.push({
267: 			type: "ask_user",
268: 			toolId: q.toolId,
269: 			questions: q.questions as RelayMessage extends { type: "ask_user" }
270: 				? never
271: 				: unknown[],
272: 		} as RelayMessage);
273: 	}
274: 
275: 	return messages;
276: }
277: 
278: // ─── Client count message factory ────────────────────────────────────────────
279: 
280: export function createClientCountMessage(count: number): RelayMessage {
281: 	return { type: "client_count", count };
282: }

125:  * Returns without error for unrecognised handler names (logged only).
126:  */
127: export async function dispatchMessage(
128: 	deps: HandlerDeps,
129: 	clientId: string,
130: 	handler: string,
131: 	payload: Record<string, unknown>,

138: 			`   [ws] client=${clientId} session=${deps.sessionMgr.getActiveSessionId() ?? "?"} Unhandled: ${handler}`,
139: 		);
140: 	}

<content>1: // ─── Relay Stack ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // The complete relay wiring: OpenCode client, SSE consumer, event translator,
3: // WebSocket handler, session manager, permission/question bridges.
4: //
5: // Extracted from skeleton.ts so integration tests exercise the exact same
6: // wiring as production. skeleton.ts is now a thin CLI wrapper around this.
7: 
8: import { stat } from "node:fs/promises";
9: import { createRequire } from "node:module";
10: import { homedir } from "node:os";
11: import { join, resolve } from "node:path";
12: import { type ClientInitDeps, handleClientConnected } from "./client-init.js";
13: import { formatErrorDetail, RelayError } from "./errors.js";
14: import { createTranslator } from "./event-translator.js";
15: import { dispatchMessage, type HandlerDeps } from "./handlers/index.js";
16: import { getClientIp, parseCookies } from "./http-utils.js";
17: import { generateSlug } from "./ipc-protocol.js";
18: import { MessageCache } from "./message-cache.js";
19: import { OpenCodeClient } from "./opencode-client.js";
20: import { PermissionBridge } from "./permission-bridge.js";
21: import { PtyManager, type PtyUpstream } from "./pty-manager.js";
22: import type { PushNotificationManager } from "./push.js";
23: import { QuestionBridge } from "./question-bridge.js";
24: import { RelayServer } from "./server.js";
25: import { SessionManager } from "./session-manager.js";
26: import { SessionOverrides } from "./session-overrides.js";
27: import { SSEConsumer } from "./sse-consumer.js";
28: import { wireSSEConsumer } from "./sse-wiring.js";
29: import { ToolContentStore } from "./tool-content-store.js";
30: import type { PartType, ProjectRelayConfig, ToolStatus } from "./types.js";
31: import { WebSocketHandler } from "./ws-handler.js";
32: 
33: // ─── WebSocket library for upstream PTY connections ─────────────────────────
34: const requireWs = createRequire(import.meta.url);
35: const wsLib = requireWs("ws");
36: const WebSocketClass = wsLib.WebSocket as typeof import("ws").WebSocket;
37: type RawData = import("ws").RawData;
38: 
39: /** Per-project relay: all relay components attached to a shared server. */
40: export interface ProjectRelay {
41: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
42: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
43: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
44: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
45: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
46: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
47: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
48: 	/** Gracefully stop relay components (SSE + WebSocket). Does NOT stop the HTTP server. */
49: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
50: }
51: 
52: // ─── Full Stack Config ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
53: 
54: export interface RelayStackConfig {
55: 	port: number;
56: 	host?: string;
57: 	opencodeUrl: string;
58: 	pin?: string;
59: 	projectDir: string;
60: 	slug: string;
61: 	staticDir?: string;
62: 	/** TLS certificate and key for HTTPS mode */
63: 	tls?: { key: Buffer; cert: Buffer; caRoot?: string };
64: 	/** Session title for the initial session */
65: 	sessionTitle?: string;
66: 	/** Log function — defaults to console.log, pass () => {} to silence */
67: 	log?: (...args: unknown[]) => void;
68: 	/** Optional pre-initialized push notification manager */
69: 	pushManager?: PushNotificationManager;
70: }
71: 
72: // ─── Stack ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
73: 
74: export interface RelayStack {
75: 	server: RelayServer;
76: 	wsHandler: WebSocketHandler;
77: 	sseConsumer: SSEConsumer;
78: 	client: OpenCodeClient;
79: 	sessionMgr: SessionManager;
80: 	translator: ReturnType<typeof createTranslator>;
81: 	permissionBridge: PermissionBridge;
82: 	questionBridge: QuestionBridge;
83: 
84: 	/** The port the HTTP server is actually listening on (useful when port=0) */
85: 	getPort(): number;
86: 	/** The base URL of the relay server */
87: 	getBaseUrl(): string;
88: 	/** Stop all components */
89: 	stop(): Promise<void>;
90: }
91: 
92: // ─── Create Per-Project Relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
93: 
94: /**
95:  * Create a per-project relay that attaches to an existing HTTP server.
96:  *
97:  * Sets up all relay components (OpenCode client, SSE consumer, translator,
98:  * session manager, bridges, WebSocket handler) and wires the full event
99:  * pipeline. Does NOT create or manage an HTTP server — the caller owns it.
100:  *
101:  * Used by both `createRelayStack()` (for standalone/skeleton mode) and the
102:  * daemon (which has its own HTTP server).
103:  */
104: export async function createProjectRelay(
105: 	config: ProjectRelayConfig,
106: ): Promise<ProjectRelay> {
107: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
108: 
109: 	// ── Components ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
110: 
111: 	const client = new OpenCodeClient({
112: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
113: 		directory: config.noServer ? config.projectDir : undefined,
114: 	});
115: 	const translator = createTranslator();
116: 	const permissionBridge = new PermissionBridge();
117: 	const questionBridge = new QuestionBridge();
118: 	const sessionMgr = new SessionManager({ client });
119: 
120: 	// ── Per-session event cache (identical to claude-relay's doSendAndRecord) ──
121: 	const cacheDir = join(
122: 		config.projectDir ?? process.cwd(),
123: 		".opencode-relay",
124: 		"sessions",
125: 	);
126: 	const messageCache = new MessageCache(cacheDir);
127: 	messageCache.loadFromDisk();
128: 
129: 	const toolContentStore = new ToolContentStore();
130: 
131: 	// Per-session overrides (agent, model, processing timeout)
132: 	const overrides = new SessionOverrides();
133: 
134: 	// ── PTY sessions with server-side scrollback (claude-relay architecture) ──
135: 	// Each active PTY gets one upstream WebSocket to OpenCode's /pty/:id/connect.
136: 	// Output is buffered server-side (50 KB FIFO per terminal) and broadcast to
137: 	// ALL browser clients. New clients get scrollback replayed on connect.
138: 	// Input from any browser client is forwarded to the shared upstream WS.
139: 	// PTYs persist across browser show/hide toggles — only closed on explicit
140: 	// pty_close (tab X button) or upstream disconnect.
141: 
142: 	const ptyManager = new PtyManager({ log });
143: 
144: 	async function connectPtyUpstream(
145: 		ptyId: string,
146: 		cursor: number = 0,
147: 	): Promise<void> {
148: 		// cursor=0 : replay entire PTY buffer (use for newly-created PTYs so the
149: 		//            initial shell prompt is delivered).
150: 		// cursor=-1: skip buffered data (use when reconnecting to an existing PTY
151: 		//            to avoid replaying stale escape sequences).
152: 		const httpUrl = new URL(
153: 			`/pty/${ptyId}/connect?cursor=${cursor}`,
154: 			config.opencodeUrl,
155: 		);
156: 		const wsUrl = httpUrl.toString().replace(/^http/, "ws");
157: 		const headers = client.getAuthHeaders();
158: 
159: 		return new Promise<void>((resolve, reject) => {
160: 			const upstream = new WebSocketClass(wsUrl, {
161: 				headers,
162: 				// Disable per-message compression for PTY connections.
163: 				// Bun's WS may negotiate permessage-deflate with Node's ws, and
164: 				// decompression bugs or flush mismatches can corrupt binary PTY data.
165: 				perMessageDeflate: false,
166: 				// Skip UTF-8 validation for TEXT frames. Terminal data can contain
167: 				// 8-bit characters (C1 control codes) that are valid in terminal
168: 				// protocols but technically invalid in strict UTF-8. Without this,
169: 				// the ws library would close the connection on such frames.
170: 				skipUTF8Validation: true,
171: 			});
172: 
173: 			// Register session BEFORE any events can fire. This mirrors the
174: 			// original pattern where the PtySession object was created before
175: 			// the WebSocket — prevents the race condition where OpenCode sends
176: 			// replay data in the same TCP segment as the upgrade response
177: 			// (Node.js ws processes both synchronously).
178: 			ptyManager.registerSession(ptyId, upstream as unknown as PtyUpstream);
179: 			let connected = false;
180: 
181: 			const connectTimeout = setTimeout(() => {
182: 				// Remove the session on timeout — it was never successfully opened
183: 				ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
184: 				reject(new Error(`Timed out connecting to PTY ${ptyId}`));
185: 			}, 10_000);
186: 
187: 			// Install message handler BEFORE "open" fires.
188: 			upstream.on("message", (data: RawData) => {
189: 				// OpenCode sends cursor metadata as binary: 0x00 + JSON {"cursor":N}.
190: 				// Always convert to Buffer first and check byte 0.
191: 				let buf: Buffer;
192: 				if (Buffer.isBuffer(data)) {
193: 					buf = data;
194: 				} else if (typeof data === "string") {
195: 					buf = Buffer.from(data);
196: 				} else {
197: 					buf = Buffer.from(data as ArrayBuffer);
198: 				}
199: 
200: 				if (buf.length > 0 && buf[0] === 0x00) return;
201: 				const text = buf.toString();
202: 				if (!text) return;
203: 
204: 				// Buffer scrollback via PtyManager (FIFO, 50 KB cap)
205: 				ptyManager.appendScrollback(ptyId, text);
206: 
207: 				// Broadcast to all browser clients
208: 				wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "pty_output", ptyId, data: text });
209: 			});
210: 
211: 			upstream.on("close", () => {
212: 				ptyManager.markExited(ptyId, 0);
213: 				// Only broadcast pty_exited if the session is still tracked.
214: 				// closeSession deletes + broadcasts pty_deleted, so we must
215: 				// not also broadcast pty_exited for the same PTY.  Similarly,
216: 				// if connectPtyUpstream failed the session was never stored.
217: 				if (ptyManager.hasSession(ptyId)) {
218: 					wsHandler.broadcast({
219: 						type: "pty_exited",
220: 						ptyId,
221: 						exitCode: 0,
222: 					});
223: 				}
224: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream closed: ${ptyId}`);
225: 			});
226: 
227: 			upstream.on("error", (err: Error) => {
228: 				if (!connected) {
229: 					clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
230: 					// Remove the failed session
231: 					ptyManager.closeSession(ptyId);
232: 					reject(err);
233: 				}
234: 				log(`   [pty] Upstream error ${ptyId}: ${err.message}`);
235: 			});
236: 
237: 			upstream.on("open", () => {
238: 				connected = true;
239: 				clearTimeout(connectTimeout);
240: 				resolve();
241: 			});
242: 		});
243: 	}
244: 
245: 	// ── Health check ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
246: 
247: 	await client.getHealth();
248: 	log(`   ✓ OpenCode is reachable at ${config.opencodeUrl}`);
249: 
250: 	// ── Session ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
251: 
252: 	const sessionId = await sessionMgr.initialize(
253: 		config.sessionTitle ?? "Relay Session",
254: 	);
255: 	log(`   ✓ Using session: ${sessionId}`);
256: 
257: 	// ── WebSocket handler ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
258: 
259: 	const wsHandler = new WebSocketHandler(
260: 		config.noServer ? null : config.httpServer,
261: 		{
262: 			// In noServer mode, the daemon's upgrade handler checks auth before
263: 			// calling handleUpgrade(). Skip verifyClient to avoid double auth.
264: 			verifyClient: config.noServer ? undefined : config.verifyClient,
265: 		},
266: 	);
267: 
268: 	const clientInitDeps: ClientInitDeps = {
269: 		wsHandler,
270: 		client,
271: 		sessionMgr,
272: 		messageCache,
273: 		overrides,
274: 		ptyManager,
275: 		permissionBridge,
276: 		questionBridge,
277: 		log,
278: 	};
279: 
280: 	wsHandler.on("client_connected", ({ clientId }) => {
281: 		log(`   [ws] Client connected: ${clientId}`);
282: 		handleClientConnected(clientInitDeps, clientId).catch((err) =>
283: 			log(
284: 				`   [ws] Client init failed for ${clientId}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`,
285: 			),
286: 		);
287: 	});
288: 
289: 	wsHandler.on("client_disconnected", ({ clientId }) => {
290: 		log(`   [ws] Client disconnected: ${clientId}`);
291: 	});
292: 
293: 	// ── SSE consumer ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
294: 
295: 	const sseConsumer = new SSEConsumer({
296: 		baseUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
297: 		authHeaders: client.getAuthHeaders(),
298: 	});
299: 
300: 	// ── Wire session manager → WebSocket ────────────────────────────────────
301: 
302: 	sessionMgr.on("broadcast", (msg) => {
303: 		// Augment session_switched with cached events (combined protocol)
304: 		if (msg.type === "session_switched") {
305: 			const switchId = (msg as { id?: string }).id;
306: 			if (switchId) {
307: 				const events = messageCache.getEvents(switchId);
308: 				const hasChatContent =
309: 					events?.some(
310: 						(e) => e.type === "user_message" || e.type === "delta",
311: 					) ?? false;
312: 				if (events && hasChatContent) {
313: 					wsHandler.broadcast({ ...msg, events });
314: 					return;
315: 				}
316: 			}
317: 		}
318: 		wsHandler.broadcast(msg);
319: 	});
320: 	sessionMgr.on("session_changed", async ({ sessionId: sid }) => {
321: 		translator.reset();
322: 		// Rebuild translator state from REST API so it knows which parts already
323: 		// exist in the session we're switching to (prevents duplicate tool_start).
324: 		try {
325: 			const messages = await client.getMessages(sid);
326: 			const parts: Array<{
327: 				parts?: Array<{
328: 					id: string;
329: 					type: PartType;
330: 					state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
331: 				}>;
332: 			}> = messages.map((m) => ({
333: 				parts: (m as { parts?: unknown[] }).parts as
334: 					| Array<{
335: 							id: string;
336: 							type: PartType;
337: 							state?: { status?: ToolStatus };
338: 					  }>
339: 					| undefined,
340: 			}));
341: 			translator.rebuildStateFromHistory(parts);
342: 		} catch {
343: 			// Non-fatal — translator will treat all parts as new
344: 		}
345: 	});
346: 
347: 	// ── Handle incoming messages from browser ───────────────────────────────
348: 	// Messages are serialized: each handler completes before the next starts.
349: 	// Without this, concurrent async handlers (e.g. new_session + switch_session)
350: 	// race — createSession's broadcast can arrive on the client AFTER
351: 	// a switch_session reply, clearing messages the user just switched to.
352: 
353: 	const handlerDeps: HandlerDeps = {
354: 		wsHandler,
355: 		client,
356: 		sessionMgr,
357: 		messageCache,
358: 		permissionBridge,
359: 		questionBridge,
360: 		overrides,
361: 		ptyManager,
362: 		toolContentStore,
363: 		config,
364: 		log,
365: 		connectPtyUpstream,
366: 	};
367: 
368: 	let _messageQueue = Promise.resolve();
369: 
370: 	wsHandler.on("message", ({ clientId, handler, payload }) => {
371: 		_messageQueue = _messageQueue.then(async () => {
372: 			try {
373: 				await dispatchMessage(handlerDeps, clientId, handler, payload);
374: 			} catch (err) {
375: 				log(`   [ws] Error handling ${handler}:`, formatErrorDetail(err));
376: 				wsHandler.sendTo(
377: 					clientId,
378: 					RelayError.fromCaught(err, "HANDLER_ERROR").toMessage(),
379: 				);
380: 			}
381: 		});
382: 	});
383: 
384: 	// ── SSE event wiring (translate → filter → cache → broadcast) ──────────
385: 
386: 	wireSSEConsumer(
387: 		{
388: 			translator,
389: 			sessionMgr,
390: 			messageCache,
391: 			permissionBridge,
392: 			questionBridge,
393: 			overrides,
394: 			toolContentStore,
395: 			wsHandler,
396: 			pushManager: config.pushManager,
397: 			log,
398: 		},
399: 		sseConsumer,
400: 	);
401: 
402: 	await sseConsumer.connect();
403: 
404: 	// ── Permission/question timeout checks ──────────────────────────────────
405: 
406: 	const timeoutTimer = setInterval(() => {
407: 		const timedOutPerms = permissionBridge.checkTimeouts();
408: 		for (const id of timedOutPerms) {
409: 			wsHandler.broadcast({
410: 				type: "permission_resolved",
411: 				requestId: id,
412: 				decision: "timeout",
413: 			});
414: 		}
415: 		const timedOutQuestions = questionBridge.checkTimeouts();
416: 		for (const id of timedOutQuestions) {
417: 			questionBridge.remove(id);
418: 			wsHandler.broadcast({ type: "ask_user_resolved", toolId: id });
419: 		}
420: 	}, 30_000);
421: 
422: 	// Don't let the timer keep the process alive
423: 	if (
424: 		timeoutTimer &&
425: 		typeof timeoutTimer === "object" &&
426: 		"unref" in timeoutTimer
427: 	) {
428: 		timeoutTimer.unref();
429: 	}
430: 
431: 	// ── Return project relay ────────────────────────────────────────────────
432: 
433: 	return {
434: 		wsHandler,
435: 		sseConsumer,
436: 		client,
437: 		sessionMgr,
438: 		translator,
439: 		permissionBridge,
440: 		questionBridge,
441: 
442: 		async stop() {
443: 			clearInterval(timeoutTimer);
444: 			overrides.dispose();
445: 			ptyManager.closeAll();
446: 			await sseConsumer.disconnect();
447: 			wsHandler.close();
448: 		},
449: 	};
450: }
451: 
452: // ─── Create Full Stack (Server + Relay) ─────────────────────────────────────
453: 
454: /**
455:  * Create a full relay stack with its own HTTP server.
456:  *
457:  * Creates a RelayServer, registers the project, starts the server, then
458:  * delegates to `createProjectRelay()` for all relay wiring. Used by
459:  * skeleton.ts for standalone operation.
460:  */
461: export async function createRelayStack(
462: 	config: RelayStackConfig,
463: ): Promise<RelayStack> {
464: 	const log = config.log ?? console.log;
465: 
466: 	// ── Push notification manager ────────────────────────────────────────────
467: 
468: 	let pushMgr: PushNotificationManager | undefined = config.pushManager;
469: 	if (!pushMgr) {
470: 		try {
471: 			const { PushNotificationManager } = await import("./push.js");
472: 			pushMgr = new PushNotificationManager();
473: 			await pushMgr.init();
474: 		} catch {
475: 			pushMgr = undefined;
476: 		}
477: 	}
478: 
479: 	// ── HTTP server ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
480: 
481: 	const server = new RelayServer({
482: 		port: config.port,
483: 		host: config.host,
484: 		pin: config.pin || undefined,
485: 		staticDir: config.staticDir,
486: 		tls: config.tls,
487: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
488: 	});
489: 
490: 	server.addProject({
491: 		slug: config.slug,
492: 		directory: config.projectDir,
493: 		title: config.slug,
494: 	});
495: 
496: 	await server.start();
497: 
498: 	const maybeServer = server.getHttpServer();
499: 	if (!maybeServer) {
500: 		throw new Error("HTTP server not available after start()");
501: 	}
502: 	// Assign to a fresh const so TypeScript narrows to non-null in closures.
503: 	const httpServer = maybeServer;
504: 
505: 	// ── Multi-project relay management ──────────────────────────────────────
506: 	// All relays use noServer mode. A single upgrade handler routes WebSocket
507: 	// connections to the correct relay by URL path (/ws → initial, /p/{slug}/ws → project).
508: 	// This matches the daemon pattern and allows dynamic project addition.
509: 
510: 	const relays = new Map<string, ProjectRelay>();
511: 	const pendingSlugs = new Set<string>();
512: 
513: 	const getProjectList = () =>
514: 		server.getProjects().map((p) => ({
515: 			slug: p.slug,
516: 			title: p.title,
517: 			directory: p.directory,
518: 		}));

520: 	/** Create a new project relay and register it. */
521: 	async function addProjectRelay(
522: 		directory: string,
523: 	): Promise<{ slug: string; title: string; directory: string }> {
524: 		// Expand ~ and resolve to absolute path
525: 		if (directory.startsWith("~/") || directory === "~") {
526: 			directory = directory.replace("~", homedir());
527: 		}
528: 		directory = resolve(directory);
529: 
530: 		// Check if directory is already registered
531: 		for (const p of server.getProjects()) {
532: 			if (p.directory === directory) {
533: 				return { slug: p.slug, title: p.title, directory: p.directory };
534: 			}
535: 		}
536: 
537: 		// Validate directory exists on disk
538: 		const dirStat = await stat(directory).catch(() => null);
539: 		if (!dirStat?.isDirectory()) {
540: 			throw new Error(
541: 				dirStat
542: 					? `Not a directory: ${directory}`
543: 					: `Directory does not exist: ${directory}`,
544: 			);
545: 		}
546: 
547: 		const existingSlugs = new Set(relays.keys());
548: 		const slug = generateSlug(directory, existingSlugs);
549: 		const parts = directory.replace(/\\/g, "/").split("/").filter(Boolean);
550: 		const title = parts[parts.length - 1] ?? "project";
551: 
552: 		// Guard against concurrent creation for the same slug
553: 		if (relays.has(slug) || pendingSlugs.has(slug)) {
554: 			const existing = relays.get(slug);
555: 			if (existing) return { slug, title, directory };
556: 			throw new Error(`Relay for ${directory} is still being created`);
557: 		}
558: 
559: 		pendingSlugs.add(slug);
560: 		try {
561: 			// Create relay FIRST — if this throws, nothing is registered
562: 			const newRelay = await createProjectRelay({
563: 				httpServer,
564: 				opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
565: 				projectDir: directory,
566: 				slug,
567: 				noServer: true,
568: 				sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
569: 				log,
570: 				getProjects: getProjectList,
571: 				addProject: addProjectRelay,
572: 				pushManager: pushMgr,
573: 			});
574: 
575: 			// Only register AFTER relay is successfully created
576: 			relays.set(slug, newRelay);
577: 			server.addProject({ slug, directory, title });
578: 
579: 			log(`   [project] Added: ${title} (${slug}) → ${directory}`);
580: 		} catch (err) {
581: 			// Clean up on failure — no zombie entries
582: 			relays.delete(slug);
583: 			log(`   [project] Failed to add ${directory}: ${formatErrorDetail(err)}`);
584: 			throw err;
585: 		} finally {
586: 			pendingSlugs.delete(slug);
587: 		}
588: 
589: 		return { slug, title, directory };
590: 	}
591: 
592: 	// ── Initial project relay ───────────────────────────────────────────────
593: 
594: 	const relay = await createProjectRelay({
595: 		httpServer,
596: 		opencodeUrl: config.opencodeUrl,
597: 		projectDir: config.projectDir,
598: 		slug: config.slug,
599: 		sessionTitle: config.sessionTitle,
600: 		log,
601: 		noServer: true,
602: 		getProjects: getProjectList,
603: 		addProject: addProjectRelay,
604: 		pushManager: pushMgr,
605: 	});
606: 	relays.set(config.slug, relay);
607: 
608: 	// ── WebSocket upgrade handler ───────────────────────────────────────────
609: 	// Routes connections by URL: /p/{slug}/ws → project relay, /ws → initial relay.
610: 	// Also checks auth when a PIN is configured (fixes pre-existing gap where
611: 	// standalone WS connections bypassed PIN auth).
612: 
613: 	httpServer.on("upgrade", (req, socket, head) => {
614: 		// Auth check (mirrors server.ts private checkAuth)
615: 		const auth = server.getAuth();
616: 		if (auth.hasPin()) {
617: 			const cookies = parseCookies(req.headers.cookie ?? "");
618: 			const sessionCookie = cookies.relay_session;
619: 			const cookieOk = sessionCookie
620: 				? auth.validateCookie(sessionCookie)
621: 				: false;
622: 			if (!cookieOk) {
623: 				const pinHeader = req.headers["x-relay-pin"];
624: 				const pinOk =
625: 					typeof pinHeader === "string" &&
626: 					auth.authenticate(pinHeader, getClientIp(req)).ok;
627: 				if (!pinOk) {
628: 					socket.destroy();
629: 					return;
630: 				}
631: 			}
632: 		}
633: 
634: 		// Route /p/{slug}/ws → project relay
635: 		const projectMatch = req.url?.match(/^\/p\/([^/]+)\/ws(?:\?|$)/);
636: 		if (projectMatch) {
637: 			const target = relays.get(projectMatch[1]!);
638: 			if (target) {
639: 				target.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
640: 			} else {
641: 				socket.destroy();
642: 			}
643: 			return;
644: 		}
645: 
646: 		// Route /ws → initial relay
647: 		if (req.url === "/ws" || req.url?.startsWith("/ws?")) {
648: 			relay.wsHandler.handleUpgrade(req, socket, head);
649: 			return;
650: 		}
651: 
652: 		socket.destroy();
653: 	});
654: 
655: 	const urls = server.getUrls();
656: 	log(`   ✓ Server listening: ${urls.local}`);
657: 
658: 	return {
659: 		server,
660: 		wsHandler: relay.wsHandler,
661: 		sseConsumer: relay.sseConsumer,
662: 		client: relay.client,
663: 		sessionMgr: relay.sessionMgr,
664: 		translator: relay.translator,
665: 		permissionBridge: relay.permissionBridge,
666: 		questionBridge: relay.questionBridge,
667: 
668: 		getPort() {
669: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
670: 			if (typeof addr === "object" && addr) return addr.port;
671: 			return config.port;
672: 		},
673: 
674: 		getBaseUrl() {
675: 			const addr = httpServer.address();
676: 			const port = typeof addr === "object" && addr ? addr.port : config.port;
677: 			const protocol = config.tls ? "https" : "http";
678: 			return `${protocol}://127.0.0.1:${port}`;
679: 		},
680: 
681: 		async stop() {
682: 			for (const r of relays.values()) {
683: 				try {
684: 					await r.stop();
685: 				} catch {
686: 					/* ignore */
687: 				}
688: 			}
689: 			relays.clear();
690: 			await server.stop();
691: 		},

<content>1: // ─── ToolContentStore ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // In-memory store for full tool result content (before truncation).
3: // Keyed by tool ID, with optional session-based grouping for bulk cleanup.
4: // Evicts oldest entries when over capacity.
5: 
6: interface Entry {
7: 	toolId: string;
8: 	content: string;
9: 	sessionId?: string;
10: 	/** Monotonic insertion order for eviction. */
11: 	order: number;
12: }
13: 
14: export class ToolContentStore {
15: 	private readonly entries = new Map<string, Entry>();
16: 	private readonly maxEntries: number;
17: 	private orderCounter = 0;
18: 
19: 	constructor(maxEntries = 500) {
20: 		this.maxEntries = maxEntries;
21: 	}
22: 
23: 	/** Store full content for a tool ID, optionally associated with a session. */
24: 	store(toolId: string, content: string, sessionId?: string): void {
25: 		// If overwriting, remove old entry first (don't double-count)
26: 		this.entries.delete(toolId);
27: 
28: 		this.entries.set(toolId, {
29: 			toolId,
30: 			content,
31: 			sessionId,
32: 			order: this.orderCounter++,
33: 		});
34: 
35: 		// Evict oldest if over capacity
36: 		while (this.entries.size > this.maxEntries) {
37: 			this.evictOldest();
38: 		}
39: 	}
40: 
41: 	/** Retrieve stored content by tool ID. */
42: 	get(toolId: string): string | undefined {
43: 		return this.entries.get(toolId)?.content;
44: 	}
45: 
46: 	/** Remove all entries associated with a session. */
47: 	clearSession(sessionId: string): void {
48: 		for (const [toolId, entry] of this.entries) {
49: 			if (entry.sessionId === sessionId) {
50: 				this.entries.delete(toolId);
51: 			}
52: 		}
53: 	}
54: 
55: 	/** Number of stored entries. */
56: 	get size(): number {
57: 		return this.entries.size;
58: 	}
59: 
60: 	/** Evict the entry with the lowest order (oldest). */
61: 	private evictOldest(): void {
62: 		let oldestKey: string | undefined;
63: 		let oldestOrder = Number.POSITIVE_INFINITY;
64: 
65: 		for (const [key, entry] of this.entries) {
66: 			if (entry.order < oldestOrder) {
67: 				oldestOrder = entry.order;
68: 				oldestKey = key;
69: 			}
70: 		}
71: 
72: 		if (oldestKey !== undefined) {
73: 			this.entries.delete(oldestKey);
74: 		}
75: 	}
76: }

<content>1: // ─── Content Truncation ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // Utilities for truncating large tool result content before relay to clients.
3: // Full content is preserved separately in ToolContentStore for on-demand fetch.
4: 
5: import type { RelayMessage } from "./shared-types.js";
6: 
7: /** Default truncation threshold in characters (~50KB). */
8: export const TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD = 50_000;
9: 
10: /** Suffix appended to truncated content. */
11: const TRUNCATION_SUFFIX = "\n\n[truncated]";
12: 
13: export interface TruncateResult {
14: 	content: string;
15: 	isTruncated: boolean;
16: 	fullContentLength?: number;
17: }
18: 
19: /**
20:  * Truncate content if it exceeds the threshold.
21:  * Returns the (possibly truncated) content, a flag, and the original length
22:  * when truncated.
23:  */
24: export function truncateContent(
25: 	content: string,
26: 	threshold = TRUNCATION_THRESHOLD,
27: ): TruncateResult {
28: 	if (content.length <= threshold) {
29: 		return { content, isTruncated: false };
30: 	}
31: 
32: 	// When the threshold is smaller than the suffix, just slice to threshold.
33: 	const suffixFits = threshold > TRUNCATION_SUFFIX.length;
34: 	const truncated = suffixFits
35: 		? content.slice(0, threshold - TRUNCATION_SUFFIX.length) + TRUNCATION_SUFFIX
36: 		: content.slice(0, threshold);
37: 
38: 	return {
39: 		content: truncated,
40: 		isTruncated: true,
41: 		fullContentLength: content.length,
42: 	};
43: }
44: 
45: /** A tool_result RelayMessage (narrowed). */
46: type ToolResultMessage = Extract<RelayMessage, { type: "tool_result" }>;
47: 
48: export interface TruncateToolResultResult {
49: 	/** The (possibly truncated) tool_result message. */
50: 	truncated: ToolResultMessage;
51: 	/** The full original content, or undefined if no truncation occurred. */
52: 	fullContent: string | undefined;
53: }
54: 
55: /**
56:  * Process a tool_result RelayMessage, truncating its content if over threshold.
57:  * Returns the modified message and the full content (if truncated).
58:  */
59: export function truncateToolResult(
60: 	msg: ToolResultMessage,
61: ): TruncateToolResultResult {
62: 	const { content, isTruncated, fullContentLength } = truncateContent(
63: 		msg.content,
64: 	);
65: 
66: 	if (!isTruncated) {
67: 		return { truncated: msg, fullContent: undefined };
68: 	}
69: 
70: 	return {
71: 		truncated: {
72: 			...msg,
73: 			content,
74: 			isTruncated: true,
75: 			fullContentLength,
76: 		},
77: 		fullContent: msg.content,
78: 	};
79: }
